mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales directly captures consumer demand flows; higher demand sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS month-over-month changes (Spearman rank correlation < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals a weakening labor market that erodes household income expectations and compresses consumption forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are jointly elevated, the real policy rate restrains aggregate demand while input cost pressures squeeze margins; this interaction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) does not exhibit a significantly negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day forward returns, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative when the signal is active.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a rising federal funds rate increases the equity risk premium and discount rate, overwhelming earnings growth and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 3.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerating above a 2% three-month annualized threshold signals improving manufacturing momentum that lifts cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month change > 2.0, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the annualized return during triggers is below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts proxies broad-based demand expansion; positive values of this interaction term drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal (joint positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS and HOUST) does not produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP expanding faster than its long-run trend signals excess aggregate demand that lifts corporate sales and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 100 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand that drags cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines by more than 100k units in a month, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the median forward return is not negative.
